headline: PJM market monitor opposes waivers for Constellation’s Three Mile Island nuclear restart
summary: Constellation needs the waivers from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to fully deliver power as soon as the unit restarts, possibly next year. It has a 20-year deal to sell all the energy, capacity and clean energy attributes to Microsoft for data centers.

headline: Constellation Energy’s Crane Loan Highlights Nuclear Role In AI Power Demand
summary: US Department of Energy approves a US$1b loan for Constellation Energy (NasdaqGS:CEG) to restart the Crane nuclear plant. Restarted facility is expected to add meaningful carbon free generation capacity to support growing electricity needs. Loan approval arrives as AI data centers and other digital infrastructure drive heavier power demand in key US regions.
